reasoning: The bullish case rests entirely on NARROW tech sector dominance in thin premarket volume, compounded by rising VIX — a dangerous combination. XLK is +1.37% while XLF sits flat at +0.19% and XLE neutral at +0.29%. This is NOT broad risk-on; it's a tech-only spike lacking financial/energy participation. When sector breadth diverges this sharply, rallies top quickly. Worse: VIX is rising 4.43% into the open, which historically signals uncertainty and mean reversion. The overnight gap of +0.86 is modest (0.12%), and premarket high 701.99 could easily be a local noise spike in thin volume that gets rejected when RTH volume enters at 09:30. Internals agent themselves admit 'lacks multi-sector breadth for high-conviction bullish day' and cap their own confidence at 0.58. The ribbon structure is technically BULL but only 80¢ spread (0.11% on a $700 stock) — not massive. This setup is fragile.

invalidation_scenarios.scenario: XLK reverses and turns negative or drops > 0.5% by 09:45 ET
invalidation_scenarios.effect: Breaks the tech leadership narrative that is driving the entire rally. Without tech momentum, there is no bullish prop. Financials and energy show zero conviction, so a tech stall collapses the whole case.

invalidation_scenarios.scenario: Premarket high 701.99 is tested on open and rejected (first RTH bar closes RED at or below 700.50)
invalidation_scenarios.effect: Invalidates the level-thesis breakout narrative and confirms premarket high was noise in thin volume, not genuine accumulation. Sets up a fade back to 699.94 (gap-fill).

invalidation_scenarios.scenario: VIX continues rising above 19.5 within first 30 minutes of RTH
invalidation_scenarios.effect: Indicates real money is de-risking and uncertainty is growing, directly invalidating the macro 'MID regime supports trend' thesis. Rising VIX into open typically signals buyers should exit, not enter.

weakest_consensus_link: Narrow sector leadership (tech only) without financial/energy confirmation. Technicals agent says CLEAN_BULL, but only 80¢ spread (0.11%) is borderline. Internals agent admits low conviction (0.58) due to lack of multi-sector breadth. If tech momentum stalls, entire rally collapses.
key_observations: XLK +1.37% vs XLF +0.19% and XLE +0.29% = DIVERGENT sector breadth, not broad risk-on
VIX rising 4.43% into the open is a historical warning sign for premarket reversals; consensus treats it as 'caution' but rising vol suggests uncertainty, not conviction
Overnight gap +0.86 is modest (0.12%); premarket high 701.99 is thin-volume noise, easy to fade on RTH open
Ribbon 80¢ spread is technically BULL but only 0.11% separation; vulnerable to quick mean-reversion rotation
Internals agent (0.58 confidence) explicitly flags 'narrow tech leadership without broad sector confirmation' — consensus agrees on the weakness but still bullish; contradiction
